# Scorched-Earth Erdős Sweep — Turn 130

This is turn 130. Turns 1 through 129 are consolidated in `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` (attached). Re-read it before answering.
Also re-read `mega_prompt.md` and `problem_statement.md`.

CRITICAL — use ONLY the files attached to THIS message. Do NOT open, import, retrieve, or cite any file from your File Library / previously uploaded files, even one named similarly (e.g. another `turns_history…md`). Other unrelated problems use similarly named history files in the same account, and importing one will silently switch you onto the wrong problem. If the attached `turns_history__frontiermath_q2_galois.md` appears missing, do NOT continue from partial context. Reply only with `MISSING_REQUIRED_ATTACHMENT` and name the missing file(s); never substitute a library file.

If this turn drifts into hammering one specific case or sub-instance, step back and ask whether the case is revealing a structural pattern that would close many cases at once. Prefer arguments whose conclusion is a uniform statement.

End this response with ESTABLISHED / GAP / NEXT + tag.
Continue with Turn 130.

## Turn 130 — all-finite counts reconstruct the profinite group

The candidate remains unchanged:

```text
sigma,tau,x0,x1

sigma2 = sigma^40491355905
u0 = (x0*tau)^40491355905
u1 = (x1*tau)^40491355905
d0 = u0*x0^-1
z0 = x0^sigma2
c0 = d0^-1*z0^-1*d0*z0

x0^2*x1^4*u1^-1*(x1^sigma)*c0
```

The tame relation is implicit. Mathematically, the exponent is the genuine idempotent \(\omega _2\in\widehat{\mathbb Z}\); \(40491355905\) is its required residue modulo \(85667662080\).  

Turn 129 audited the last two non-combinatorial inputs to the source-free certificate theorem. One final logical bridge had not been stated explicitly: equality of all finite surjection counts must yield an actual profinite-group isomorphism, rather than merely a verifier-equivalent counting rule. 

### 1. The submission defines a genuine profinite group

Let \(\mathcal Q_{A_2}\) be the cofiltered category of finite groups equipped with a generating marked tuple
\[
(s,t,y_0,y_1)
\]
such that
\[
t^s=t^2,\qquad y_0,y_1\in O_2(G),
\]
and the displayed wild word evaluates to \(1\), with all exponentiation interpreted using the genuine \(\omega _2\).

Two marked quotients have a common refinement: take the subgroup of their direct product generated by the paired marked generators. The normal closure of its paired \(y_0,y_1\) lies in the product of the two \(2\)-cores and is therefore a finite \(2\)-group.

Define
\[
\Gamma_{A_2}:=\varprojlim_{(G;s,t,y_0,y_1)\in\mathcal Q_{A_2}}G.
\]

The compatible marked tuples give elements
\[
\sigma,\tau,x_0,x_1\in\Gamma_{A_2}.
\]
They topologically generate \(\Gamma_{A_2}\), since their images generate every defining finite quotient. Moreover, the closed normal subgroup
\[
P=\overline{\langle\!\langle x_0,x_1\rangle\!\rangle}
\]
is pro-\(2\): its image in every defining finite quotient lies in that quotient’s \(2\)-core.

Consequently, for every finite group \(G\),
\[
\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},G)
\]
is naturally identified with the generating tuples counted by the submission semantics. Thus the source-free certificate theorem is genuinely a theorem about finite quotients of a four-generated profinite group.

### 2. Reconstruction lemma

**Lemma.** Let \(A\) and \(B\) be profinite groups, with \(A\) topologically finitely generated. Suppose \(A\) and \(B\) have exactly the same finite quotients. Then \(A\cong B\).

First construct an epimorphism \(A\twoheadrightarrow B\). For each open normal subgroup \(N\triangleleft B\), let
\[
X_N=\operatorname{Sur}(A,B/N).
\]
These sets are nonempty and finite. In the product \(\prod_NX_N\), impose the compatibility equations induced by quotient maps.

Every finite collection of these equations is solvable: intersect the finitely many relevant \(N\)’s, choose a surjection onto the resulting finite quotient, and compose. Compactness therefore supplies a compatible family, hence a continuous map
\[
A\longrightarrow\varprojlim_N B/N=B
\]
surjective on every finite quotient. Its image is compact and dense, hence equals \(B\).

It follows that \(B\) is also topologically finitely generated. Reversing the argument gives an epimorphism \(B\twoheadrightarrow A\).

Finally, every finitely generated profinite group is Hopfian. Indeed, if \(\phi:A\twoheadrightarrow A\), then for each open normal \(N\), the subgroups
\[
N,\phi^{-1}(N),\phi^{-2}(N),\ldots
\]
all have the same finite index. There are only finitely many open normal subgroups of that index, and inverse image under a surjection is injective on subgroups; hence some positive power satisfies
\[
\phi^{-k}(N)=N.
\]
Therefore \(\ker\phi\subseteq N\) for every open normal \(N\), so \(\ker\phi=1\).

The composition
\[
A\twoheadrightarrow B\twoheadrightarrow A
\]
is therefore an automorphism, forcing the first epimorphism to be injective and hence an isomorphism.

### 3. Application to \(G_{\mathbb Q_2}\)

The frozen certificate theorem gives
\[
\left|\operatorname{Sur}(\Gamma_{A_2},G)\right|
=
\left|\operatorname{Sur}(G_{\mathbb Q_2},G)\right|
\]
for every finite group \(G\). In particular, the two profinite groups have exactly the same finite quotients.

Since \(\Gamma_{A_2}\) is four-generated, the reconstruction lemma applies and gives
\[
\boxed{\Gamma_{A_2}\cong
\operatorname{Gal}(\overline{\mathbb Q}_2/\mathbb Q_2)}.
\]

Thus the certificate theorem, if its audited recurrences and determinant calculation are accepted, proves not only verifier-count equality but that the displayed relation is an actual profinite presentation.

**ESTABLISHED**: Constructed the genuine four-generated profinite group represented by the submission’s \(2\)-core semantics. Proved that a topologically finitely generated profinite group is determined by its finite quotient set, via compactness, mutual epimorphisms, and Hopficity. Therefore the frozen all-finite surjection-count theorem implies an actual isomorphism \(\Gamma_{A_2}\cong G_{\mathbb Q_2}\), closing the proof-to-presentation bridge.

**GAP**: The private 5402-group verifier remains unrun, and the assembled recurrence proof has not received an external line-by-line manuscript review.

**NEXT**: Submit the frozen candidate unchanged; any reported failure should be localized through the certificate DAG to its first failing recurrence node.
